As you know, your company has been selected as one of the suppliers for the [Project Name] Packaging Optimization Project. To support this project, we would like to invite you to a training session on [Date], from [Start Time] to [End Time], via Teams. The training will be conducted in English and is designed to introduce the [System Name] Compliance System.

Regarding your question about the original text: Yes, the English paragraph is grammatically correct and flows smoothly. It is clear, professional, and appropriate for a business invitation. The only minor improvement would be adding "is" before "designed" (i.e., "...and **it is** designed..." or simply "...and **designed**..."), but the original version is still understandable.
